decisions.reasoning: We need to establish settlements to start collecting resources. The WHEAT on 11 (2 pips) tile is the most productive, giving 2 wheat on a roll of 11. The tile is also not adjacent to any existing settlements, satisfying the distance rule. Building here maximizes resource collection early in the game.

As the first player, I should establish a road to connect my settlement and explore potential settlement spots. Building a road from node 0 to node 1 is the most strategic choice as it opens up the board and allows for future expansion.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, so rolling a 10 or 11 will prevent production. Building a settlement on a wheat-producing tile is the most valuable action, as it provides the most resources. Settlement 9 provides wheat on 3 and 6, which are both likely rolls.

The game just started, and I need to expand my network to connect to my second settlement. Building a road from node 12 to node 13 is the best option as it connects to my second settlement and expands my network.

As the first player, I should establish a strong starting position by placing settlements on high-probability resource tiles. The WHEAT on 11 and WHEAT on 3 tiles are both high-probability (2 pips each) and have a 3:1 port, which is very beneficial. The WHEAT on 11 tile is closer to my starting settlement, making it a safer choice for the first turn.

This tile produces for both players, and moving the robber there will steal from the opponent and prevent them from collecting resources. The opponent has 2 brick cards, so this action is effective.
